text: The patient is positioned supine in the Trendelenburg position, with the head lower than the feet, to facilitate surgical access to the colorectal area.
During laparoscopic exploration, a small bowel loop is observed to be incarcerated into the femoral canal on the right side.
Three 8mm robotic trocars are positioned for the procedure using the DaVinci XI robotic system.
The DaVinci XI robotic system is positioned and docked from the patient's left side, preparing for the surgical procedure.
The bedside surgeon is positioned on the patient's right side during the procedure.
No assistant trochar is inserted during the procedure, with the surgical setup involving three eight-millimeter robotic trochars and the patient in a supine Trendelenburg position.
The surgeon performs a peritoneal incision between the medial umbilical ligament and the right anterior superior iliac spine, using robotic instruments to access the surgical site.
The surgeon creates a peritoneal flap, manipulating the tissue to separate it from underlying structures.
The surgeon performs dissection of the angel's hair, a delicate tissue layer, using precise surgical techniques.
The surgeon performs dissection of the preparitoneal area using monopolar core re-mounted robotic scissors, carefully manipulating the tissue within the surgical field.
The surgeon dissects the hernia sac medial to the epigastric vessels, carefully separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon performs an incision medial to the femoral ring in the lacunar ligament to facilitate the reduction of the incarcerated bowel loop.
The surgeon reduces the bubble loop, which is visible within the surgical field.
The surgeon completes the reduction of the hernia sac, which is visibly repositioned within the surgical field.
The right round ligament is visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon demonstrates the femoral ring, highlighting its anatomical location and relation to surrounding structures.
The surgeon assesses bowel viability by observing the vascularization after administering cyanine green dye intravenously, highlighting areas of perfusion in the bowel tissue.
The surgeon demonstrates bowel vascularization, likely using imaging techniques to visualize blood flow within the bowel tissue.
The surgeon inserts a proline mesh into the preperitoneal space, positioning it to reinforce the area following the reduction of the hernia sac.
The surgeon fixes the proline mesh to the abdominal wall using cyanoacrylate glue, securing it in place within the surgical field.
The surgeon closes the peritoneal incision using a V-lock suture, completing the operation.
